title: ranunculus montanus subsp. carinthiacus
intro: Ranunculus montanus subsp. carinthiacus, also known as Alpine buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus montanus plant, belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. The Alpine buttercup has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which are known to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus montanus subsp. carinthiacus include tea infusions, infusions in oil, and extracts, which can be used to create ointments and salves for topical application.
